title: router-contract.v1
description: A constrained routing decision for Laguna M.1 acting as a router/planner, written by the laguna-task-contract skill to .laguna/router-contract.json at the workspace root. M.1 produces skill calls and bounded task contracts — never free-form plans or recursive delegation. Each delegations[].task_contract must itself validate against task-contract.schema.json; that cross-schema rule is enforced by the validator (scripts/validate_contract.ts), not by a $ref, because the skill's zero-dependency schema interpreter does not dereference $ref.

schema_version.description: Contract identifier; always the literal string "router-contract.v1".

model_mode.description: Always "laguna_m_router": this contract is produced by Laguna M.1 as a constrained router. Worker contracts belong in task-contract.v1.

user_goal.description: The user's request, condensed but faithful. This field may contain unbounded language (it is the user's words); the bounded-contract rules apply to the delegated goals, not here.

candidate_skills.description: The closed menu of skills the router chose among. Routing is a choice from this list, not open space.

chosen_skill.description: Exactly one skill, taken from candidate_skills (the validator enforces membership).

reason.description: One or two sentences grounding the choice in the request's evidence.

delegations.description: Bounded work orders handed to XS.2 workers. No recursion: a delegation embeds a task contract, and task contracts cannot contain delegations.

worker_model.description: Delegations always target Laguna XS.2 as the bounded worker.

task_contract.description: A complete task-contract.v1 object (see task-contract.schema.json). The validator validates each embedded contract against that schema and applies the same bounded-contract rules to it.

stop_conditions.description: When the routed work is finished or abandoned. Must include both "validator_passed" (the success exit) and "escalation_required" (the explicit failure-is-acceptable exit); the validator enforces this.
